name: SLB N.V.

profile.description: SLB (formerly Schlumberger) is the world's largest oilfield services company, providing technology and integrated solutions across drilling, reservoir characterization, production, and digital platforms. Q1 2026 revenue was approximately $8.5B with 2.7% YoY growth. The company operates in 100+ countries with 109,000 employees and has invested heavily in digital and energy transition technologies.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): SLB has maintained technology leadership in oilfield services but revenue growth of 2.7% is modest. AI Deployment (1/2): Digital platform with AI-enhanced drilling and reservoir modeling. Market Share (2/2): World's largest oilfield services company operating in 100+ countries [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Digital and energy transition technologies expanding. R&D Intensity (0/1): Standard for oilfield services.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Technology-driven energy solutions across 100+ countries. Community Impact (1/2): Sustainability and energy transition investments. Employee Retention (1/2): 109,000 employees with strong global employer brand. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Physical oilfield services across 100+ countries are immune to AI disruption. AI enhances but cannot replace drilling, completion, and production operations [1]. AI Opportunity (0/1): Digital platform generates operational efficiency but no separately quantified AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: SLB's 100-year history and global technology leadership demonstrate institutional durability. The energy transition investments show forward thinking.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Technology-driven energy solutions — functional purpose [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): SLB explicitly benchmarks against Halliburton and Baker Hughes to improve.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Investing in energy transition and digital technologies beyond traditional oilfield services [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): Standard industry operations management.

investment_thesis: SLB offers a 2.15% dividend yield at 16.4x forward P/E as the world's largest oilfield services company with a conservative 42.5% D/E and 9.3% profit margin [1]. Global operations across 100+ countries provide diversified revenue and market leadership.
thesis_continued: Revenue growth of only 2.7% YoY and PEG of 1.89 suggest limited growth relative to valuation [2]. The energy transition creates long-term structural uncertainty for oil services. Profit margin of 9.3% is thin and cyclically vulnerable.
key_catalysts: Energy transition technologies creating new revenue streams
key_risks: Oil price decline compressing customer spending [1]
Energy transition reducing fossil fuel demand long-term
PEG of 1.89 suggesting limited growth at current valuation
sources: SLB Financial Results and Digital Strategy Updates — SEC Filings, 2026
Yahoo Finance — SLB Key Statistics, Dividend Data, accessed June 2026
